title: Share project by email + pending invite for unregistered users
description: Let a project owner initiate a share by entering a recipient email. Create a project_invites row associated with an existing user if the email matches one, or a pending invite (invitee_user_id NULL) if not, without ever silently dropping an invite to an unregistered address.
acceptance_criteria: project_invites table (id, project_id, inviter_user_id, invitee_email, invitee_user_id nullable, status, created_at, resolved_at) added via the additive migration pattern
Sharing to a registered user's email immediately sets invitee_user_id and the invite becomes visible to that user
Sharing to an unregistered email creates a pending invite with invitee_user_id NULL that is not lost or expired silently
Only the project owner can initiate a share for their own project (enforced by the ownership helper from FEATURE-1031/1033)
An invited-but-not-accepted recipient sees only the project name and a short summary, never full project contents, before accepting
notes: See docs/adr/0006-project-sharing-invites-and-cloning.md. Depends on EPIC-103 (ownership) and EPIC-102 (users table).
